description: If something can't be, it was never possible.

hints.text: A clean interaction between □ and ◇: if $\lnot A$ holds in every accessible world, then there cannot be an accessible world where $A$ holds. The proof uses `diamond-def` to unfold ◇ into its □-form, then derives a contradiction. Start by assuming $\Box \lnot A$ and then assume $\Diamond A$ for a [reductio-ad-absurdum|reductio].
Assume $\Diamond A$ — we'll derive a contradiction.
Apply `diamond-def` to unfold $\Diamond A$ into $\lnot \Box \lnot A$.
Use `not-elim` on step 3 to unpack the [negation|negation]: $\Box \lnot A \to \bot$.
[modus-ponens|Modus ponens]: the conditional at step 4 applied to step 1 gives $\bot$.
Discharge $\Diamond A$ to get $\Diamond A \to \bot$, i.e. $\lnot \Diamond A$.
